title: allium nigrum subsp. multibulbosum
intro: Allium nigrum subsp. multibulbosum, also known as Black Garlic or Black Raisin Garlic, is a subspecies of the Allium nigrum plant, characterized by its dark, almost black cloves and strong, sweet flavor. Traditionally, Black Garlic has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant and antibacterial effects, which can help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Black Garlic include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. Black Garlic can be prepared through various herbal methods, including fermentation, drying, and powdering, which allows for its active compounds to be easily incorporated into teas, tinctures, and supplements.
